steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Use the scale shortcut: find the linear ratio
steps.content:
Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$, triangle $SKL \sim$ triangle $STR$ (AA similarity). The linear scale factor is:
$$\frac{LK}{RT} = \frac{28}{84} = \frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Recover ST from the area
steps.content:
Right triangle area $= \frac{1}{2} \cdot RT \cdot ST$:
$$1,050 = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 84 \cdot ST \implies ST = 25$$

steps.title: Apply scale factor to find SK, then KT
steps.content:
Corresponding sides scale by $\frac{1}{3}$, so $SK = \frac{1}{3} \cdot 25 = \frac{25}{3}$.
$$KT = ST - SK = 25 - \frac{25}{3} = \frac{50}{3} \approx 16.667$$
The answer is 50/3.
